frozen_components: LLaMA3-70B (used for summarization and QA synthesis, not trained)
7B taxonomy extraction model (pre-trained, used for structure identification)
architecture.summary: StructTuning is a two-stage domain knowledge injection system for LLMs. In Stage 1 (SCPT), a knowledge taxonomy is automatically extracted from domain corpora using LLaMA3-70B for chunk summarization and a specialized 7B model for hierarchy identification. Training chunks are then prepended with their corresponding knowledge paths (rendered as mindmaps via templates) and used for conditioned language modeling, with models also trained to reconstruct the full hierarchy. In Stage 2 (SSFT), knowledge-intensive and multi-hop QA pairs are synthesized via LLaMA3-70B using random walks over the taxonomy; answers are enriched with explicit knowledge paths and chain-of-thought prompts to teach models to apply learned structure to solve problems.
trained_components: LLM parameters during SCPT (conditioned pre-training on textual chunks with prepended knowledge paths)
LLM parameters during SSFT (supervised fine-tuning on synthesized QA pairs with explicit knowledge structure in outputs)

biblio.title: Structure-aware Domain Knowledge Injection for Large Language Models

claims.statement: achieves 100% of traditional knowledge injection performance with only 5% of training corpus
using merely 76M tokens (0.3%) achieves over 50% performance improvement against MMedLM (which uses 25.5B tokens)
with 1.2B tokens (5% of corpus) achieves nearly 100% of MMedLM's improvement (4.95 percentage points v.s. 4.96)
on LongBench closed-book QA, SCPT+SSFT achieves 31.5% recall versus 24.2% for CPT+SFT baseline
generalizes across model architectures: InternLM2-7B (+4.46%), Llama2-7B (+8.78%), Llama2-13B (+6.17%) using 76M tokens
SSFT with additional 8K structure-aware QA pairs demonstrates cross-lingual knowledge transfer, where knowledge injected in one language helps solve problems in other languages

RAG strategy does not bring significant advantages to MMedBench evaluation (38.12% on English), underperforming SCPT+SSFT (49.96%), due to gap between pre-training corpus (textbook knowledge) and evaluated QA samples (practical diagnosis records)
two-stage strategy introduces added computational complexity through taxonomy extraction, data reorganization in SCPT phase, and optional QA synthesis in SSFT phase
